title: Completeness Report
description: The orchestrator's derive/validate response. Extends the validator's {legal, complete, violations[], summary} verdict with a choice-completeness view: awaiting_choices plus a typed manifest[]. FLAG-ONLY: the manifest names WHAT choice is missing/over — a generic resource KIND plus counts — and NEVER an options pool, a source-specific name, or any source content (liability principle). Pre-1.0, may break until F07 closes.

schema_version.description: Report contract version. Optional in light v1; becomes required at 1.0.

legal.description: No illegal (or internal) findings — the sheet breaks no rule.

complete.description: Rules-only: nothing expected-but-missing (no incomplete/internal findings). Independent of awaiting_choices — a sheet can be legal + complete + awaiting_choices.

awaiting_choices.description: True iff the manifest holds at least one entry with status 'required' — the sheet is otherwise fine but still needs the user to pick something.

manifest.description: Typed completeness entries, ordered by section (core → grimoire → inventory → companion). Diff-able across stateless calls because choice_key is deterministic (not server state).

violation.description: A validator finding — mirrors validator/report.py's Violation output shape.

manifestEntry.description: One completeness issue — a missing or over-filled choice. FLAG-ONLY: it names the generic KIND to choose and the counts, never an options pool or source-specific name.

choice_key.description: Stable, deterministic public id — a readable 'section.path' string, e.g. 'core.classes.0.subclass'. Identical across stateless calls for the same field, so a UI can track a single choice's progress.

section.description: The schema section the choice belongs to. Manifest is ordered by this.

path.description: Field path within the section, e.g. 'classes[0].subclass' or 'skills'.

resource.description: The generic KIND of thing to choose — never a source-specific name and never accompanied by an options list (liability principle).

type.description: The issue: nothing chosen where required (missing), under a required count (too_few), or over a cap (too_many).

status.description: Whether this pick is mandatory (drives awaiting_choices) or an available-but-optional one.

description.description: Human-readable message. Non-authoritative convenience only — all machine logic keys off the typed fields above.

count.description: Quantities behind the issue. filled is always present; required/min/max appear as the issue type warrants (required or min for under-fills, max for over-fills).

filled.description: How many the sheet currently holds.
